extraction.one_line_summary: CARAPAZ Richard attacked in the final kilometers of the 20km climb to Peñas Blancas, dropped SOLER Marc and HAGEN Carl Fredrik for his first Vuelta stage win.
tactical_patterns: 32-rider breakaway established early on flat coastal roads when all teams recognized GC wouldn't contest 120-mile stage with summit finish.
KELDERMAN Wilco and POELS Wout leapt from outside top-20 to 6th and 9th overall, exploiting GC riders' reluctance to chase massive break.
UAE placed three riders in break including SOLER Marc, showing tactical shift to stage hunting over GC defense after losing main domestique.
RODRIGUEZ Carlos cracked final 300 meters despite attacking intent, hip hematoma from earlier crash likely limiting power output on 20km ascent.

rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: won stage after disappointing Giro, proving climbing form intact despite questioning himself after poor GC performance earlier in Vuelta
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: third place finish signals strong form, podium at Tour de France preceded by Tour de Suisse win shows pattern of peaking mid-Grand Tour
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: gained 14+ GC positions to 6th overall by infiltrating breakaway, demonstrates ability to exploit tactical complacency of favorites
rider_signals.RODRÍGUEZ Carlos: negative: visible deterioration final 300m with hip hematoma suggests crash damage will compromise performance in upcoming weekend mountain stages
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: defended red jersey conservatively after losing VINE Jay domestique, took no risks on only descent showing defensive posture
